author: Jack | Will Never Message You First
msg:
Apple is very specific on their use cases that they allow for background permissions. "BGContinuedProcessingTask" in particular should be initiated by user action and isn't meant to be a persistent activity.

There are alternatives that we're looking into for a more reliable connection on iOS

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg: The attestation issues are intentional - They are a requirement put in place externally, and the level required varies depending on several factors. It's not a blanket application to the whole system. We have worked with our partners to plan a system that works for legitimate devices with out having Google as a 3rd party requirement. This won't necessarily solve all attestation issues though - Different levels of attestation are required depending on many factors, and some profiles will require levels that only locked, unrooted, and factory setups will match.

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
It's difficult to say, as there are many factors. Root/bootloader unlock are not possible with the current Google-backed attestation method. Again - this is a known issue and something we're actively working to solve. The upcoming implementation for Android will only require a Google approved device, and a TEE/StrongBox chip - which includes the vast majority of Android 7+ devices. Again though, our partners may ask for certain traffic or devices to match different profiles, which may require an unrooted device, or locked bootloader to pass. This will be made more transparent to the user through dedicated error messaging.

With the current implementation, there's no difference between the APK/AppStore version for attestation. Both are valid options. This will continue to be so.

author: R B

author: Kolks
msg: Since when is a TEE/StrongBox chip a requirement?

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg: It is not a requirement to run Unity. It may be a requirement to earn incentives through the telemetry task

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
Android 6.0 (2015) has it as a requirement for any device implementing a secure lock screen. So from ~2015 onward, any device that ships with Google Play (i.e., passes CTS/GMS certification) must have a functioning TEE.

Again, we understand it's not a perfect solution, but we can't make everyone happy in this instance. This is a requirement outside of the engineering team's control, and we've done our best to remove as many limitations while still providing necessary security and confidence.

author: hidrex
msg:
It is the Scout and Runner module where users:
- submit SIM cards and calling packages,
- map which countries/routes those packages actually cover,
- get those submissions approved/moderated,
- earn rewards for valid package data.

Basically, it is the telecom package database-building layer that feeds Scout and Runner.

author: Charles
msg:
SoC (System on a Chip)
TEE (Trusted Execution Environment)

author: Charles
msg: Stuff inside your device

author: Abdus
msg: Thanks

author: Abdus
msg: What did I miss about TEE and SoC?

author: Abdus
msg: Is there a reason it is being brought up?

author: GliTch_
msg: seems a new security requirement / layer for some of the task issuers

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
SoC stands for "System on a Chip" - You can think of it as whole computer on a single chip.

TEE stands for "Trusted Execution Environment" - It's a dedicated "piece" of the SoC that's isolated from the rest of the "computer" - it runs it's own operating system, and acts as a kind of "vault" where communications between it and the rest of the "computer" are tightly controlled and restricted.

A popular example of a TEE is Apple's Secure Enclave

author: TelecomTom
msg: Facts

author: TelecomTom
msg: Why was my message deleted? For stating facts?

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - The Global Physical Compute Marketplace**

Unity Network already operates one of the largest composable, people powered edge network on the planet with more than 40,000 devices online across 170+ countries in 1,500+ cities, performing Proof-of-Work and generating rewards.

The Unity Network Global Physical Compute Marketplace allows anyone - businesses, developers, AI agents - to post tasks that run on your devices. The same edge network that powers the network today will now serve demand from every industry that needs real devices in real locations.

If a company is launching an app in South Africa, they need to know what their users actually experience - not what a data centre simulation tells them. If someone is monitoring prices across 20 countries, they need real devices loading real pages. If they need to verify that something physically exists at a specific location, they need a real phone with real sensors in that place.

No VPN can fake this. No cloud server can replicate it. Only real devices in real hands in real locations - and that is exactly what Unity License Operators provide every single day.

How it works - a buyer posts a task to the marketplace and specifies where it needs to run - which countries, which carriers, how many devices. The Unity Network's gateway matches the task to available devices and dispatches it. Your device executes, returns the result, and you earn rewards. The entire cycle takes seconds.

Three ways buyers access the network:

- Marketplace website - for non-technical users who want to point and click
- API - for developers building Unity Network's functionality into their own platforms
- MCP - for AI agents that autonomously dispatch tasks to non synthetic devices

What this means for operators:

More demand flowing through the network means more tasks, which means more reward potential. Higher value tasks earn proportionally higher rewards. The more consistently your device is online, the higher your reputation score, and the more premium work you receive.

Keep your device online. Keep your app updated. More details on specific task categories coming in future announcements.

🔗 https://x.com/unitynodesio/status/2044531364330872839

🤝 **Official Unity** **Resources**
